description_long: This is a tall, narrow-necked ceramic vase with a single curved handle attached from the upper neck to the shoulder. The surface is decorated with hand-painted blue motifs, featuring birds perched among stylized floral and leafy patterns, reminiscent of traditional Delftware or Iznik pottery. The body is bulbous, tapering smoothly to a small, flat base, and the mouth is slightly flared. The handle is robust and painted with a spotted pattern, providing both visual interest and functional grip. The glaze has a matte to semi-gloss finish, with visible brushwork and some minor surface irregularities. The overall color palette is dominated by off-white and deep blue, giving the vase a classic, antique appearance.
description: A tall, bulbous ceramic vase with a single handle, decorated in blue bird and floral motifs on an off-white background. The surface is hand-painted and slightly matte, evoking a traditional, antique style.
description_view_0: Front view showing two prominent blue birds and floral motifs, with the handle partially visible on the right.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the handle and a side bird motif, with more of the neck and mouth visible.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the handle's attachment and the continuation of the floral pattern, with less bird detail visible.
description_view_3: Side view with the handle fully visible and a partial bird motif, showing some minor surface marks.
